proofs.claim: smoke_run FAILS CLOSED on a numeric eval id instead of silently recording null evidence and exiting 0.
proofs.cmd: printf '%s' '{"scope":"pr","tier":1,"tier_justification":"t","head_sha":"x","evals":[{"id":1,"mode":"scripted","priority":"P0","cmd":"true","negative_control":"false","evidence":"e"}]}' > /tmp/p1_numid.json; ./scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-run /tmp/p1_numid.json > /tmp/p1_out.txt 2>&1; test $? -ne 0 && grep -q 'non-string id' /tmp/p1_out.txt && echo "P1 exit=0 refused-as-expected"
proofs.expect: exit 0 — smoke-run returns non-zero and names the non-string id, rather than exiting 0 with null smoke

proofs.claim: The normal string-id path still executes and records REAL evidence, so the guard did not break honest use.
proofs.cmd: printf '%s' '{"scope":"pr","tier":1,"tier_justification":"t","head_sha":"x","evals":[{"id":"1","mode":"scripted","priority":"P0","cmd":"true","negative_control":"false","evidence":"e"}]}' > /tmp/p2_strid.json; ./scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-run /tmp/p2_strid.json > /dev/null 2>&1 && test "$(jq -r '.evals[0].smoke.cmd_exit')" = "0" && test "$(jq -r '.evals[0].smoke.negative_control_exit')" = "1" && echo "P2 exit=0 real-evidence-recorded"
proofs.expect: exit 0 — cmd_exit 0 and negative_control_exit 1 actually observed and written

proofs.claim: The post_evals test suite passes with the guard in place.
proofs.cmd: bash hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -q '^PASS$' && echo "P3 exit=0"
